evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Uses rave-python with Rave() init and rave.Card.verify(tx_ref) - this is internally consistent with rave-python SDK conventions. flutterwave-react-v3 with useFlutterwave hook is consistent. Comment claims .Card.verify works for mobile money which is questionable; rave-python is also less maintained vs direct REST. Long-tail consistent, no version pinned.
evidence.executability: Fenced blocks parse cleanly. Backend imports correct. Frontend uses react-router-dom v6 syntax correctly. No sandbox-infeasible patterns (no ngrok/sudo). Minor: needs both .env files set up, but template is provided in prose not as a fenced .env.example block.
evidence.completeness: (a) keys acquisition described; (b) install commands present; (c) env vars described in prose but NO explicit .env.example fenced block - violates explicit requirement; (d) backend code present; (e) frontend code present; (f) test commands present. Missing structured .env.example block is a notable gap.
evidence.citation_anchoring: No URLs cited anywhere in the playbook - no link to developer.flutterwave.com or any docs.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable: scored on internal consistency. No version pins on pip/npm packages (violates requirement b). .env.example not in a fenced block as required.
